value: The numeric claim "78% of employers use language tests" remains unsupported by a primary public source.
The blanket competitor framing is contradicted because Pipplet and Allingua are multilingual and not English-only.
The absolute uniqueness claim is contradicted because Luxembourg-based multilingual testing and reporting already exists.

independent_operator_examples.name: Allingua
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Local multilingual provider with Luxembourg-linked language testing and training footprint.

independent_operator_examples.name: Pipplet
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Commercial assessment platform relevant to the audit use case, but not a fragmented local operator.

independent_operator_examples.name: Pearson Versant
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Assessment benchmark proving substitute tooling exists, not a small private operator layer.

pricing_or_offer_proof: Current evidence proves substitute tools and providers, but not a clean public price layer for specialist language audits.
value.social_funnel_signal: No Facebook-group-led funnel signal is captured yet in the validated evidence set.

segment_weighting.rationale: B2B product. Operator proof relevance: medium. Social funnel relevance: low. Broad private providers count, but weaker than niche independent operators.

value.takeaway: The need is commercially legible, but the visible market is dominated by testing platforms and broader providers, not by a clear fragmented operator layer.
value.why_not_stronger: Current evidence proves substitute tools, not a cluster of small local audit specialists.

independent_operator_summary.omission_reason: Derived independent-operator signal for prioritization and further market research. Keep it out of customer-facing copy until translated into product-safe language.
